sector_rotation.explanation: Cyclicals are outperforming defensives, suggesting investors are comfortable with risk. Typically seen in early/mid expansion phases.

news_headlines.title: Trump’s plan for $500 million Spirit Airlines bailout draws GOP division - The Washington Post
news_headlines.description: The president said this week that bailing out the airline would save jobs, but his administration is divided.

news_headlines.title: 7-0 wipeout: I put ChatGPT-5.5 vs Claude 4.7 through 7 impossible tests — and the results shocked me - Tom's Guide
news_headlines.description: Only one model deserves the hype

news_headlines.title: Google's $40B Anthropic move is Big Tech's latest huge AI bet - Axios
news_headlines.description: The AI lab is their competition and their bet

news_headlines.title: Musk Drops Fraud Claims Against OpenAI, Altman Ahead of Trial - Bloomberg.com
news_headlines.description: Elon Musk dropped his fraud claims against OpenAI and co-founders Sam Altman and Greg Brockman, narrowing the scope of his lawsuit against his business rivals on the eve of trial.

news_headlines.title: ‘Shot across the bow’: How Washington plans to take on prediction markets - Politico
news_headlines.description: Lawmakers' concerns about insider trading in the prediction markets took on new life after prosecutors alleged that a U.S. soldier illegally traded on then-Venezuelan leader Nicolás Maduro's capture.

news_headlines.title: Utah medical board calls for immediate suspension of state’s AI doctor experiment - statnews.com
news_headlines.description: A Utah pilot to renew prescriptions using a bot faced backlash from the state's medical board, which called for an immediate suspension.

news_headlines.title: Intel Stock Surges 23% After Earnings, On Track for Record Close. Can AI Drive It Higher? - Barron's
news_headlines.description: Intel shares were up sharply in premarket trading Friday after the chip maker reported strong first-quarter results.

news_headlines.title: Nvidia stock closes at record, pushing market cap past $5 trillion - CNBC
news_headlines.description: Nvidia's stock closed at its first record since October, as a rally in Intel pushed chipmakers higher.

news_headlines.title: Tennessee Becomes Second State to Outlaw Bitcoin, Crypto ATMs - Decrypt
news_headlines.description: Tennessee has become the second U.S. state to outlaw Bitcoin and crypto ATMs, making it a criminal offense to own or operate the machines.

news_headlines.title: Trump extends Jones Act waiver for another 90 days - CBS News
news_headlines.description: The waiver lets international ships carry goods between U.S. ports and is aimed at lowering energy prices.

news_headlines.title: Did Mamdani’s 'tax the rich' video outside a billionaire's NYC penthouse cross a line? - Gothamist
news_headlines.description: Business leaders described the video as an insensitive invasion of privacy.

news_headlines.title: X-energy stock pops 27% on first day of trading following upsized IPO - TechCrunch
news_headlines.description: Investors flocked to nuclear power startup X-energy in its first day of public trading on the Nasdaq.

news_headlines.title: Markets News, April 24, 2026: Nasdaq, S&P 500 Hit Records as Intel Soars; Oil Falls on US-Iran Talks; DOJ Drops Powell Probe - Investopedia
news_headlines.description: Stocks ended mostly in the green Friday, powered by soaring shares of Intel, with the Nasdaq Composite and S&P 500 finishing higher for a fourth straight week after setting their latest intraday and closing records.

news_headlines.title: This is a ‘come to Jesus moment’: Ford CEO says American carmakers are battling a perfect storm - Fortune
news_headlines.description: If Ford "doesn't put its chips on the right number," it may not survive, said Farley.

news_headlines.title: Musk Admits Tesla (TSLA) Full Self-Driving Claims Were Lies - TipRanks
news_headlines.description: Tesla ($TSLA) is now under pressure after its CEO, Elon Musk, admitted that millions of customers were sold Full Self-Driving (FSD) packages on promises that could never be kept. On

nyt_headlines.title: U.S. Sanctions Zigzag in New World of Economic Warfare
nyt_headlines.abstract: With oil prices in mind, the Trump administration has deployed a haphazard approach to sanctions on Russia and Iran.

nyt_headlines.title: The Podcast Where You Can Eavesdrop on the A.I. Elite
nyt_headlines.abstract: Dwarkesh Patel was a bored college sophomore looking for intellectual stimulation. Now he commands interviews with Jensen Huang and Mark Zuckerberg and holds his own with deeply nerdy A.I. researchers.

nyt_headlines.title: How We Traced U.S. Government Gold to a Drug Cartel
nyt_headlines.abstract: Three reporters followed supply chains to reveal that the U.S. Mint buys gold that comes from foreign pawn shops and drug dealers, then claims it is from the United States.

nyt_headlines.title: These Couples Wanted to Have Children. Rising Costs Are Stopping Them.
nyt_headlines.abstract: High mortgage payments, higher child care costs and economic uncertainty are making some people rethink their plans on starting a family.

nyt_headlines.title: The Chief of Chicago’s Science Museum Is Doing Some Experiments
nyt_headlines.abstract: Chevy Humphrey explains why the scientific method matters in business.

nyt_headlines.title: Targeted Hunts Were Supposed to Curb ‘Zombie Deer Disease.’ Now What?
nyt_headlines.abstract: In Illinois and other states, officials hoped that culls could halt the progress of chronic wasting disease. Now they are losing hope.

nyt_headlines.title: How Do You Measure A.I. Firms’ Gargantuan Energy Plans? In ‘Bragawatts.’
nyt_headlines.abstract: One way for a company to stand out — or to intimidate the competition — is to boast, often without evidence, about how much power it has access to.

nyt_headlines.title: Confusion and Fright Inside the Washington Hilton Ballroom
nyt_headlines.abstract: A sense of danger spread like a wave among high-profile politicians and journalists as an emergency unfolded at the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner.

nyt_headlines.title: ‘Michael’ Shakes Off Bad Reviews to Become a Box Office Juggernaut
nyt_headlines.abstract: Lionsgate estimated on Saturday that the Michael Jackson biopic would collect more than $200 million over its first few days in theaters.

nyt_headlines.title: In Wine Country, Sales Are Down and Fraud Is Rampant
nyt_headlines.abstract: The industry’s murky supply chain has long attracted scammers and con artists. In the words of one expert, “Wine and fraud go hand in hand.”

nyt_headlines.title: Justice Department Drops Criminal Investigation Into the Fed
nyt_headlines.abstract: The decision to end the inquiry into Jerome H. Powell’s handling of the Federal Reserve’s renovation could allow Kevin M. Warsh, the president’s pick for Fed chair, to be confirmed.

nyt_headlines.title: The ‘Lasting Damage’ of Pirro’s Abandoned Fed Investigation
nyt_headlines.abstract: The Trump administration’s attacks on the Federal Reserve have rattled confidence in the central bank’s ability to operate independently before a leadership transition.

nyt_headlines.title: How Kevin Warsh Could Shrink the Fed’s Footprint in Financial Markets
nyt_headlines.abstract: President Trump’s nominee to become the next chair of the Federal Reserve wants to overhaul the central bank, including its more than $6 trillion balance sheet.

nyt_headlines.title: Tariffs Raised Consumers’ Prices, but the Refunds Go Only to Businesses
nyt_headlines.abstract: Many families felt the sting of the president’s now-illegal tariffs, but companies have said little about whether they will share the $166 billion coming back to them.

nyt_headlines.title: Justice Dept. Closes Criminal Investigation of Fed Chair
nyt_headlines.abstract: Also, the U.S. is sending top envoys to Pakistan for Iran peace talks. Here’s the latest at the end of Friday.
